name: Karisa Maitha
bio: Powerful coast political kingpin and Minister for Local Government who died suddenly while on an official state trip to Germany.

name: Ananiah Mwaboza
bio: Prominent coast lawyer who won the high-stakes 2004 mini-poll under the National Liberal Party banner, beating the preferred NARC candidate.

name: George Saitoti
bio: Long-serving Vice President, Minister for Internal Security, and mathematics professor who tragically died in a police chopper crash in the Ngong hills.

name: Moses Ole Sakuda
bio: Former administrator who captured the seat on a TNA ticket, cementing Uhuru Kenyatta's new party dominance right before the 2013 general elections.

name: Mutula Kilonzo Sr.
bio: Acclaimed Senior Counsel, former Minister for Justice and Constitutional Affairs, and the historic pioneer Senator of Makueni County.

name: Mutula Kilonzo Jr.
bio: High Court advocate who succeeded his father in a landslide by-election victory, later transitioning to become Makueni Governor in 2022.

name: Ken Okoth
bio: Beloved legislator highly celebrated across Kenya for his transformational educational projects and transparent management of the CDF kitty.

name: Bernard Imran Okoth
bio: Served as the constituency office manager under his late brother, winning the highly contested 2019 mini-poll to preserve the family's development legacy.

name: Paul Koinange
bio: Influential Chairperson of the National Assembly Departmental Committee on Administration and National Security who succumbed to illness during his term.

name: John Njuguna Wanjiku
bio: Grassroots youth leader who won a historic, razor-thin proxy battle for the United Democratic Alliance party, shifting the region's political alignment.

name: Aden Duale
bio: Veteran politician and former Leader of Majority who resigned his parliamentary seat to take up his executive appointment as Cabinet Secretary for Defence.

name: Barrow Dekow Mohamed
bio: Prominent local leader and businessman who won the subsequent by-election to retain the seat cleanly under the ruling party's banner.

name: Mirugi Kariuki
bio: Renowned human rights lawyer, multi-party democracy activist, and Assistant Minister for Internal Security who died alongside other lawmakers on a peace mission.

name: William Kariuki
bio: Son of the late Mirugi Kariuki who contested the seat at age 27, winning on a sympathy wave to serve out the remainder of his late father's term.

name: Mugabe Were
bio: Newly elected MP in the December 2007 polls who was tragically shot dead outside his Nairobi home in January 2008, sparking widespread urban protests.

name: Ferdinand Waititu
bio: Aggressive grassroots campaigner who won the tense 2008 mini-poll, launching his long career as a legislator and subsequent Kiambu County Governor.

name: Lorna Laboso
bio: First-time legislator and Assistant Minister for Home Affairs who tragically died alongside Roads Minister Kipkalya Kones in a June 2008 light aircraft crash.

name: Joyce Laboso
bio: Academic and sister to Lorna Laboso who successfully held the seat for ODM, later rising to Deputy Speaker and pioneer female Bomet Governor.

name: George Thuo
bio: Prominent transport entrepreneur and Government Chief Whip whose 2007 election victory was nullified by the High Court due to tallying irregularities.

name: William Kabogo
bio: Flamboyant businessman who bypassed traditional major parties to secure a commanding victory on a GNU ticket, paving his path to becoming Kiambu Governor.

name: Joseph Nkaissery
bio: Retired Major General who surrendered his parliamentary opposition seat to accept an executive appointment as Cabinet Secretary for Interior.

name: Elijah Memusi
bio: Switched from the ruling alliance to the opposition ODM right before the by-election, successfully keeping the crucial pastoralist seat in opposition hands.

name: Charles Keter
bio: Highly influential Rift Valley political strategist who resigned his Senate seat after being appointed Cabinet Secretary for Energy and Petroleum.

name: Aaron Cheruiyot
bio: Youthful political newcomer who overcame a stiff regional challenge to retain the seat, later ascending to become the Senate Majority Leader.

name: Cyrus Kiiru Mwangi
bio: Highly vocal Member of County Assembly who chaired the Finance Committee and was known for challenging local executive governance transparency.

name: Esther Mwihaki
bio: Former media correspondent who staged a strong independent grassroots campaign to defeat the heavily backed ruling party candidate.
